new_classes.label_en: Wine of fresh grapes, incl. fortified wines; grape must (excl. heading 2009)
new_classes.label_de: Wein aus frischen Weintrauben, einschl. mit Alkohol angereicherter Wein; Traubenmost (ausgen. Position 2009)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes, including fortified wines, and grape must other than that of heading 2009. Grape must is the liquid produced by pressing fresh grapes, including partly fermented grape must. Fortified wines are wines to which alcohol has been added to arrest fermentation or to raise the alcoholic strength.

new_classes.label_en: Production process of wine from fresh grapes or grape must
new_classes.label_de: Herstellungsprozess von Wein aus frischen Weintrauben oder Traubenmost
new_classes.definition_en: The process of fermenting fresh grapes or grape must to produce wine, including the process of fortifying wine with added alcohol, as covered by CN heading 2204.

new_classes.label_de: Qualität des Ursprungs aus frischen Weintrauben
new_classes.definition_en: A quality of a beverage indicating that it is produced from fresh grapes (Vitis vinifera), distinguishing wine-based products of heading 2204 from other fermented beverages, vermouth (2205), and other grape-derived products classified under heading 2009.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria from the legal text are: (1) the product is wine of fresh grapes (including fortified wines) or grape must; (2) grape must of heading 2009 is explicitly excluded. The new class WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204 captures the positive criteria via the FreshGrapeOriginQuality and WineFreshGrapesProduction restrictions. The exclusion of grape must of heading 2009 (fruit juices, including grape juice) cannot be fully expressed as a simple OWL restriction without an explicit sibling class for 2009 grape must being present in the TBox — this negative/exclusion criterion is noted but not formally axiomatised, reducing coverage. The additional note on 'actual alcoholic strength by volume' referenced in the legal text is an interpretive cross-reference, not a product-discriminating criterion at heading level, and is therefore not modelled as a restriction (it applies to sub-headings). Coverage is set to 0.75 to reflect the missing formal exclusion of heading 2009 grape must.
